manifest.name: Writing Studio

name: Writing Studio

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Writing Studio 2.9.0 — a maintenance release resolving three triaged issues.

### Fixed
- **PDF export engine selection (#201)** — PDF export now selects a LaTeX engine that matches the requested font (xelatex → lualatex → pdflatex), degrading gracefully to pdflatex (dropping the font, with a notice) instead of failing. When no LaTeX engine is installed, the error names the missing PDF engine and points to a LaTeX distribution (MiKTeX / TeX Live) rather than blaming Pandoc. DOCX and RTF export are unchanged.
- **Project-type document defaults (#193)** — New and added documents default to a document type matching the project type (book → chapter, series/blog → article, journal/magazine → section). The global default document type setting is now the fallback for blank projects only, and is labeled as such.

### Changed
- **Clearer input placeholders (#194)** — The new-project title placeholder is now "Name your project" instead of "My novel", and the word count goal placeholder reads "Optional (no goal if blank)" instead of a bare "50000". Example placeholders use a consistent full-word "Example:" cue, translated and sentence-cased in all 12 locales.

Split out for future releases: configurable PDF engine / wkhtmltopdf (#203) and renaming the per-project document folder (#204).

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/writerP-777/obsidian-writing-studio/compare/2.8.0...2.9.0

**Notebook Navigator integration** — If you use [Notebook Navigator](https://github.com/johansan/notebook-navigator) alongside Writing Studio, the **Open in sidebar explorer** item now appears directly in Notebook Navigator's folder context menus. The native Obsidian file explorer context menu is unchanged.

releases.author: writerP-777
releases.description:
## What's fixed

**Persistent startup delays and UI freezing under Lazy Plugin Loader**

When other plugins configured with LPL delays loaded after startup, each triggered an `active-leaf-change` event. With no debounce, every event caused a full Launcher re-render calling `getTotalWordCount()` — which reads every file in the active project from disk sequentially. On a project with many chapters, a burst of plugin-load events saturated the vault I/O queue, freezing the Settings panel and sidebar views for up to 1–2 minutes.

Two fixes:

1. `refreshLauncher()` calls from `active-leaf-change` are now debounced with a 300 ms timer, collapsing rapid bursts into a single render.
2. `getTotalWordCount()` caches its result per active project and only re-reads from disk when a file is modified or the binder is saved, making subsequent renders instant.

## Installation

Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` and place them in your vault's `.obsidian/plugins/writing-studio/` folder, then reload the plugin.

## What's Changed
* fix: debounce launcher refresh and cache word counts to eliminate LPL startup freeze (v2.4.4) by @writerP-777 in https://github.com/writerP-777/obsidian-writing-studio/pull/79


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/writerP-777/obsidian-writing-studio/compare/2.4.3...2.4.4

releases.author: writerP-777
releases.description:
## What's fixed

**Startup delay regression from v2.4.2**
The `window.setTimeout(fn, 0)` wrapper added to the `onLayoutReady` callback in v2.4.2 caused a noticeable delay on every startup. In normal load the callback fires from the `layout-ready` event, and wrapping in `setTimeout(0)` pushed all initialization work to the back of the macro-task queue — behind every other plugin's `layout-ready` handler. On vaults with many plugins this was clearly perceptible before the Launcher appeared. Reverted to the original `async` callback pattern.

The Lazy Plugin Loader stale-render fix from v2.4.2 (`refreshLauncher()` in the existing-leaf branch of `openLauncher()`) is unaffected and remains in place.

## Installation

Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` and place them in your vault's `.obsidian/plugins/writing-studio/` folder, then reload the plugin.

## What's Changed
* fix: remove setTimeout(0) startup delay regression (v2.4.3) by @writerP-777 in https://github.com/writerP-777/obsidian-writing-studio/pull/78


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/writerP-777/obsidian-writing-studio/compare/2.4.2...2.4.3

releases.author: writerP-777
releases.description:
## What's fixed

**Launcher panel stale render under Lazy Plugin Loader (first run)**
On the first Obsidian startup after configuring Lazy Plugin Loader, workspace restoration calls `LauncherView.onOpen()` before `projectManager.initialize()` has run, leaving the panel showing "no project selected." `openLauncher()` then found the existing leaf and revealed it without re-rendering, so the stale state persisted whenever `active-leaf-change` did not fire (e.g. the launcher was already the active sidebar leaf). Fixed by calling `refreshLauncher()` in the existing-leaf branch of `openLauncher()`, matching the pattern already used in `openBinder()`.

**Launcher and Binder fail to open on subsequent Lazy Plugin Loader starts**
On all runs after the first, the plugin loads after LPL's configurable delay, at which point `workspace.onLayoutReady` fires synchronously inside `onload()`. The async callback yielded at `await projectManager.initialize()`, and the microtask continuation called `getLeftLeaf(false)` while empty/placeholder leaves from the previous session were still mid-cleanup in the workspace's leaf management cycle, causing `getLeftLeaf` to return `null` and the panel to silently not open. Fixed by wrapping the `onLayoutReady` body in `window.setTimeout(fn, 0)`, moving execution to the macro-task queue so all pending workspace leaf work completes before a leaf is requested.

## Installation

Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` and place them in your vault's `.obsidian/plugins/writing-studio/` folder, then reload the plugin.

## What's Changed
* docs: update README version badge to 2.4.1 by @writerP-777 in https://github.com/writerP-777/obsidian-writing-studio/pull/75
* fix: Launcher/Binder deferred-init failures under Lazy Plugin Loader (v2.4.2) by @writerP-777 in https://github.com/writerP-777/obsidian-writing-studio/pull/77


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/writerP-777/obsidian-writing-studio/compare/2.4.1...2.4.2

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new in 2.3.0

### Internationalization — 10 languages

Writing Studio is now fully translated into 10 languages. The plugin automatically detects your Obsidian language setting and displays in the matching language, falling back to English for any unsupported locale.

**Supported languages:** English · Arabic · Chinese (Simplified) · French · German · Japanese · Korean · Portuguese (Brazil) · Russian · Spanish

**To switch languages:** Settings → General → Language in Obsidian. Restart required.

### Changes

- All plugin UI strings translated across every view, modal, and panel
- Status badges and document type labels in the Targets Dashboard and Writing Binder now display in the active language
- Word-count suffix adapts per language (e.g. 字, 語, 자, W)
- i18n key-parity test suite added — CI catches missing translations automatically
- README updated with table of contents and Language support section

### Installation

Download \main.js\, \manifest.json\, and \styles.css\ from this release and copy them to \<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-writing-studio/\.

releases.author: writerP-777
releases.description:
## [2.2.1]

### Fixed
- Remove `fs` module from `EpubEngine` — EPUB binary now written via `vault.createBinary` / `vault.modifyBinary`, eliminating the "Direct Filesystem Access" community scorecard warning
- Remove `!important` from `.ws-hidden` in `styles.css` — replaced with `body .ws-hidden` selector for equivalent specificity, eliminating the "Avoid !important" scorecard warning

### Added
- `scripts/fetch-plugin-guidelines.sh` (`npm run fetch:guidelines`) — downloads the current Obsidian plugin guidelines, developer policies, and submission requirements from the official GitHub source into `docs/obsidian-guidelines/`
- `scripts/scorecard-check.sh` (`npm run check:scorecard`) — pre-commit hook that checks source files against the cached guidelines and community scorecard patterns; blocks on regressions, warns on accepted known patterns

## What's Changed
* Release 2.2.1 — remove fs module, scorecard pre-commit hook, UI text fixes by @writerP-777 in https://github.com/writerP-777/obsidian-writing-studio/pull/52


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/writerP-777/obsidian-writing-studio/compare/2.2.0...2.2.1

## Fixed

- Replaced 18 `!important` declarations in `styles.css` with higher-specificity selectors — eliminates 18 community plugin scorecard CSS warnings
  - Typography Mode overrides now use `body.writing-studio-typography` prefix (specificity `0,2,1`), beating theme selectors at `0,2,0` without `!important`
  - Launcher button overrides now use `body .ws-launcher` prefix (specificity `0,1,2`), beating theme `.workspace button` at `0,1,1`

## Changed

- Added GitHub all-releases download count badge to README

releases.author: writerP-777
releases.description:
## What's changed

  - Fixed sentence case on six UI labels (mode switcher, binder, sprint timer, folder sidebar) to meet Obsidian plugin
  store requirements
  - Updated ESLint config to resolve rule conflict between local linter and the Obsidian review bot

  **No new features. No settings or data affected. Safe to update.**
releases.name: Writing Studio 2.1.9

releases.author: writerP-777
releases.description:
## What's changed

  This release resolves all issues flagged by the ObsidianReviewBot during community plugin review:

  - Removed a disallowed `eslint-disable` directive comment
  - Corrected sentence-case for UI strings in the mode switcher, sprint controls, launcher buttons, and export modal
  - Command name "Publish to wordpress" updated to comply with Obsidian linting rules

  No user-facing functionality has changed.
releases.name: Writing Studio 2.1.8

releases.author: writerP-777
releases.description:
What's new in 2.1.7

  New features

  - Session word count — tracks words written in the current Obsidian session, shown in
   the Launcher
  - Daily writing log — sprint summaries automatically appended to your Daily Note
  - Manuscript export — export projects to PDF, DOCX, RTF, HTML, and EPUB via Pandoc
  - Mode status bar — active writing mode (Draft, Edit, Review) shown in the Obsidian
  status bar
  - Project word count goal — set and track a total word count target per project
  - Folder sidebar explorer — enhanced folder navigation with breadcrumb trail and file
   browser

  Fixes

  - Corrected sentence case on several UI strings flagged by ObsidianReviewBot
  - Fixed "Publish to WordPress" command capitalisation

  Security

  - Added CodeQL, OpenSSF Scorecard, and ESLint GitHub Actions workflows — every push
  and PR is now scanned automatically
  - All workflow dependencies pinned to commit SHAs
  - Dependabot enabled for npm and GitHub Actions
  - Pre-commit hook runs ESLint (blocking); pre-push hook runs full CodeQL scan (blocks
   on HIGH/CRITICAL findings)
  - Fixed YAML frontmatter backslash escaping in FrontmatterManager
  - Replaced Math.random() UUID generation with crypto.randomUUID() in the EPUB engine

  Installation

  Download main.js, manifest.json, and styles.css from this release and copy them to
  <vault>/.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-writing-studio/.
releases.name: Writing Studio 2.1.7

releases.author: writerP-777
releases.description:
## What's New in 2.1.6

  ### Folder Sidebar Explorer — Major Update

  **Search**
  Search your folders and files directly from the sidebar. Type a query and press Enter to search both file and folder
  names as well as the full text content of Markdown and text files. Results appear instantly with matched terms
  highlighted. Content matches display a text snippet showing exactly where in the file the term was found. Frontmatter
  is excluded from content search to prevent false positives from YAML fields.

  **Sort**
  A sort dropdown lets you control how folders and files are ordered:
  - Folders first, A–Z (default)
  - Folders first, Z–A
  - Name A–Z
  - Name Z–A
  - Newest modified first
  - Oldest modified first

  **Insert Selection to Editor**
  When previewing a file in the sidebar, text is now selectable. Highlight any passage and click the **↩ Insert
  selection** button to insert it at the cursor position in your active editor — useful for pulling quotes, references,
  or passages from source files without leaving your draft.

  **Hover Info Tooltips**
  Hover over any file or folder in the sidebar to see an information card:
  - **Files** — last modified date and time, file size, word count
  - **Folders** — total file count, subfolder count

  Word count reflects document body only (frontmatter excluded) and loads from Obsidian's file cache.

  ---

  ### Documentation
  The README and the in-app **How to Use** tab have been updated to document all new Folder Sidebar Explorer features.
releases.name: Writing Studio 2.1.6

releases.author: writerP-777
releases.description:
### Fixed
  - All inline JavaScript style assignments replaced with CSS classes (Obsidian guidelines compliance)
  - All unhandled promises now properly awaited or handled with `void`
  - Eliminated TypeScript `any` casts — replaced with specific types throughout
  - Sentence case applied consistently across all UI text, settings, and notices
  - Settings tab headings follow Obsidian conventions
  - `normalizePath()` applied to all vault file paths, including Move to Research in Binder
  - Removed unnecessary `async` from methods with no `await`
  - Promise return types corrected where `void` was expected
  - Settings headings use correct `setHeading()` pattern

  ### Internal
  - ESLint config updated with Obsidian plugin ruleset (`eslint-plugin-obsidianmd`)
  - Full compliance pass against Obsidian community plugin review requirements
releases.name: Writing Studio 2.1.5

releases.author: writerP-777
releases.description:
What's new in 2.1.4

  Typography font picker
  Right-click inside the editor while Typography Mode is active to access a new Typography font → menu. All available fonts are listed with a checkmark on the active selection.
  Changes apply instantly without opening settings.

  Typography fonts now work for all users
  Font files were previously referenced locally and never reached installed users. Fonts are now loaded from Google Fonts and work for everyone out of the box. An internet
  connection is required on first use per font; fonts are cached afterward and work offline.

  Open on startup toggle
  A new Open on startup setting in Settings → General controls whether the Writing Studio Launcher opens automatically when Obsidian loads. Previously this was always on with no
  way to disable it.

  Single ribbon icon
  Only the feather icon (Open writing studio) now appears in the ribbon. All other feature icons that were being added automatically have been removed.

  Grouped context menus
  All Writing Studio items in every right-click menu are now grouped under a Writing studio options heading, keeping them organized and separate from Obsidian's built-in options.

  "Add to writing project" project picker
  Right-clicking a file and choosing Add to writing project now opens a modal with a dropdown listing all your projects, so you can choose which project to add the file to.

  In-settings documentation
  A new How to use tab in Settings renders the full plugin documentation inline, so instructions are always available without leaving Obsidian. The content stays automatically in
  sync with the README on every build.

  Updated README
  Full documentation rewrite covering all features, updated workflows, font options table, project template details, and accurate context menu and command references.
releases.name: Writing Studio 2.1.4

releases.author: writerP-777
releases.description:
### Fixes
- Fixed sentence case across all UI text strings
- Resolved unhandled promise warnings
- Fixed promises returned where void return was expected
- Replaced `any` types with proper TypeScript types
- Removed inline styles in favor of CSS classes
- Removed unnecessary async from methods with no await expression
- Fixed settings heading conventions
releases.name: Writing Studio 2.1.3

Initial public release of Obsidian Writing Studio.

Features include Focus Mode, Writing Binder, Sprint Timer, Typography Mode, WordPress publishing, Folder Sidebar Explorer, Export Engine, and more.
